title: IEC 62443-3-2 SIS zone-and-conduit security-level evidence: SL-T target vs SL-C capability vs SL-A achieved, with sub-clause exception register
exclusions: Excludes SIS components fully replaced via UC-22.63.5 MoC where the replacement is still in lifecycle phase 6-10 (the SL-A measurement applies post-commissioning). Excludes zones that have been formally decommissioned and de-registered.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.63.7` archived weekly to `audit_evidence` with `iec_clause="IEC-62443-3-2"`. Every zone pins (zone_id, conduit_ids, sis_id, sl_target_FR1_FR7, sl_capability_FR1_FR7, sl_achieved_FR1_FR7, sl_gap_count, sl_exception_register_ref, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: IEC 62443-3-2 (Security risk assessment for system design) defines the zone-and-conduit method and the Security Level (SL) construct (SL-T target, SL-C capability, SL-A achieved). This UC enforces the three-SL discipline across the seven Foundational Requirements (FR1 Identification and Authentication Control, FR2 Use Control, FR3 System Integrity, FR4 Data Confidentiality, FR5 Restricted Data Flow, FR6 Timely Response to Events, FR7 Resource Availability) and reports the SL-T-vs-SL-A gaps with exception-register linkage.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates SL-T-vs-SL-A conformance per zone and per Foundational Requirement.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `IEC 62443-3-2 SL Conformance` dashboard, the per-zone SL-T allocation, the per-component SL-C certificate (TÜV / exida / ISA Secure), the SL-A measurement report, the exception register.

compliance.assurance_rationale: IEC 61511 Clause 8.2.4 mandates a CRA on the SIS; 62443-3-2 provides the recognised methodology. This UC enforces the 62443-3-2 SL evidence-chain limb (zone/conduit + SL-T/SL-C/SL-A), which is part of the larger Clause 8.2.4 evidence basis tracked by UC-22.63.2.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides the 62443-3-2 SL-evidence limb of Clause 8.2.4.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An SIS zone has SL-T = (3,3,3,2,3,2,2) allocated, but the engineering-workstation in that zone is certified only to SL-C = (1,1,1,1,1,1,1) without a documented exception or compensating control. UC-22.63.7 MUST flag with risk_score >= 90.
controlTest.negativeScenario: The same zone has SL-T = (3,3,3,2,3,2,2), every in-zone component is SL-C certified to >= SL-T, and the SL-A measurement (post-commissioning system review) confirms SL-A >= SL-T. UC-22.63.7 MUST record risk_score=0.

dataSources: KV Store `iec_62443_zone_lookup` (zone_id, sis_id, sl_target_FR1_FR7). KV Store `iec_62443_component_sl_capability_lookup` (component_id, zone_id, vendor, part_number, sl_capability_FR1_FR7, certification_ref, certification_expiry). KV Store `iec_62443_sl_achieved_measurement_lookup` (zone_id, measurement_id, sl_achieved_FR1_FR7, measurement_date, assessor_ref, findings_count). KV Store `iec_62443_sl_exception_register_lookup` (exception_id, zone_id, fr_index, sl_target, sl_achieved, compensating_control_ref, business_acceptance_ref, expiry_at).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 6849).
spl:
| inputlookup iec_62443_zone_lookup
| eval frs = mvappend("FR1","FR2","FR3","FR4","FR5","FR6","FR7")
| mvexpand frs
| eval fr = frs
| eval sl_target = case(
    fr = "FR1", sl_target_FR1,
    fr = "FR2", sl_target_FR2,
    fr = "FR3", sl_target_FR3,
    fr = "FR4", sl_target_FR4,
    fr = "FR5", sl_target_FR5,
    fr = "FR6", sl_target_FR6,
    fr = "FR7", sl_target_FR7)
| join type=left zone_id [
    | inputlookup iec_62443_component_sl_capability_lookup
    | stats min(sl_capability_FR1) as sl_c_FR1, min(sl_capability_FR2) as sl_c_FR2, min(sl_capability_FR3) as sl_c_FR3, min(sl_capability_FR4) as sl_c_FR4, min(sl_capability_FR5) as sl_c_FR5, min(sl_capability_FR6) as sl_c_FR6, min(sl_capability_FR7) as sl_c_FR7 by zone_id
  ]
| eval sl_capability = case(
    fr = "FR1", sl_c_FR1,
    fr = "FR2", sl_c_FR2,
    fr = "FR3", sl_c_FR3,
    fr = "FR4", sl_c_FR4,
    fr = "FR5", sl_c_FR5,
    fr = "FR6", sl_c_FR6,
    fr = "FR7", sl_c_FR7)
| join type=left zone_id [
    | inputlookup iec_62443_sl_achieved_measurement_lookup
    | stats latest(sl_achieved_FR1) as sl_a_FR1, latest(sl_achieved_FR2) as sl_a_FR2, latest(sl_achieved_FR3) as sl_a_FR3, latest(sl_achieved_FR4) as sl_a_FR4, latest(sl_achieved_FR5) as sl_a_FR5, latest(sl_achieved_FR6) as sl_a_FR6, latest(sl_achieved_FR7) as sl_a_FR7, max(measurement_date) as last_sl_a_measurement_at by zone_id
  ]
| eval sl_achieved = case(
    fr = "FR1", sl_a_FR1,
    fr = "FR2", sl_a_FR2,
    fr = "FR3", sl_a_FR3,
    fr = "FR4", sl_a_FR4,
    fr = "FR5", sl_a_FR5,
    fr = "FR6", sl_a_FR6,
    fr = "FR7", sl_a_FR7)
| join type=left zone_id, fr [
    | inputlookup iec_62443_sl_exception_register_lookup
    | rename fr_index AS fr
    | stats latest(compensating_control_ref) as compensating_control_ref, latest(business_acceptance_ref) as business_acceptance_ref, latest(expiry_at) as exception_expiry_at by zone_id, fr
  ]
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval exception_valid = if(isnotnull(business_acceptance_ref) AND (isnull(exception_expiry_at) OR exception_expiry_at > now_ts), 1, 0)
| eval days_since_sl_a = round((now_ts - last_sl_a_measurement_at) / 86400, 1)
| eval sl_a_stale = if(days_since_sl_a > 730 OR isnull(last_sl_a_measurement_at), 1, 0)
| eval sl_c_gap = if(sl_capability < sl_target, 1, 0)
| eval sl_a_gap = if(sl_achieved < sl_target, 1, 0)
| eval unresolved_sl_gap = if((sl_c_gap = 1 OR sl_a_gap = 1) AND exception_valid = 0, 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    unresolved_sl_gap = 1 AND sl_target >= 3, 95,
    unresolved_sl_gap = 1 AND sl_target = 2, 80,
    unresolved_sl_gap = 1, 60,
    sl_a_stale = 1 AND sl_target >= 3, 70,
    sl_a_stale = 1, 40,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +sl_achieved
| table zone_id sis_id fr sl_target sl_capability sl_achieved exception_valid compensating_control_ref days_since_sl_a risk_score
description: Pivots every SIS zone across the seven Foundational Requirements (FR1-FR7) and reconciles SL-T-vs-SL-C-vs-SL-A. The minimum SL-C across all in-zone components defines the zone-SL-C; the latest formal SL-A measurement (typically a TÜV / exida / ISA Secure SL assessment) defines the zone-SL-A. Gaps without a current exception (compensating-control + business-acceptance) are risk-scored 60-95 by SL-target severity. The SL-A staleness limb prevents an organisation from claiming SL-3 forever based on a single commissioning-day measurement.
value: IEC 62443-3-2 SL-evidence is rapidly becoming a near-universal procurement / insurance / regulator requirement for new SIS deployments. The cost of a zone-SL-gap discovered post-commissioning (replacing certified components retroactively, or rearchitecting zones) is one-to-two orders of magnitude higher than the cost of catching the gap at design time (the SL-T allocation phase). This UC operates the SL-evidence chain — a key piece of the larger CRA process tracked by UC-22.63.2.
implementation: (1) Maintain `iec_62443_zone_lookup` from the CRA output (SL-T allocations). (2) Maintain `iec_62443_component_sl_capability_lookup` from vendor SL-C certificates (TÜV / exida / ISA Secure certifications; some vendors publish SL-C declarations directly). (3) Schedule a biennial SL-A measurement via an accredited 62443 assessor and write results to `iec_62443_sl_achieved_measurement_lookup`. (4) Maintain `iec_62443_sl_exception_register_lookup` for documented exceptions with compensating controls. (5) Schedule UC-22.63.7 weekly; route `risk_score >= 95` to OT Security Lead + Functional Safety Manager at severity P1.
visualization: (Panel 1) SL conformance matrix (zone x FR). (Panel 2) SL-T-vs-SL-A scatter. (Panel 3) Exception register with expiry status. (Panel 4) SL-A staleness heat map.

references.title: IEC 62443-3-2:2020 — Security risk assessment for system design
IEC 62443-3-3:2013 — System security requirements and security levels
IEC 62443-4-2:2019 — Technical security requirements for IACS components

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Vendor SL-C in re-certification**: a component vendor with an SL-C certificate in renewal may temporarily report an expired-soon certificate — load `certification_renewal_letter_ref` and treat as in-window for up to 90 days. 2. **SL-A measurement in-progress**: an SL-A measurement is underway and the latest report is still in draft — load `sl_a_measurement_in_progress_ref` to suppress for up to 60 days. 3. **Documented compensating control**: an exception with a current `compensating_control_ref` (e.g., a unidirectional gateway compensating for a missing FR5 capability) and a `business_acceptance_ref` not yet expired is treated as resolved.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `iec_61511_22_63_7_exceptions` with the OT Security Lead + Functional Safety Manager documented approval, an explicit zone_id + fr scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.
reviewer: OT Security Lead + Functional Safety Manager + IEC 62443 Certified Lead Assessor
equipmentModels: ISA Secure / TÜV / exida certified components (Siemens / Honeywell / HIMA / Emerson / Yokogawa / Schneider / ABB)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-22.63.2 (CRA register) deployed.
- A current zone-and-conduit register from the CRA output.
- Vendor SL-C certificates for every in-zone component.
- An accredited IEC 62443-3-2 / IEC 62443-3-3 assessor on retainer for biennial SL-A measurements.

## Step 1 - Zone register

```ini
[iec_62443_zone_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.zone_id = string
field.sis_id = string
field.sl_target_FR1 = number
field.sl_target_FR2 = number
field.sl_target_FR3 = number
field.sl_target_FR4 = number
field.sl_target_FR5 = number
field.sl_target_FR6 = number
field.sl_target_FR7 = number
```

## Step 2 - Component SL-C

```ini
[iec_62443_component_sl_capability_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.component_id = string
field.zone_id = string
field.vendor = string
field.part_number = string
field.sl_capability_FR1 = number
field.sl_capability_FR2 = number
field.sl_capability_FR3 = number
field.sl_capability_FR4 = number
field.sl_capability_FR5 = number
field.sl_capability_FR6 = number
field.sl_capability_FR7 = number
field.certification_ref = string
field.certification_expiry = time
field.certification_renewal_letter_ref = string
```

## Step 3 - SL-A measurement

```ini
[iec_62443_sl_achieved_measurement_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.zone_id = string
field.measurement_id = string
field.sl_achieved_FR1 = number
field.sl_achieved_FR2 = number
field.sl_achieved_FR3 = number
field.sl_achieved_FR4 = number
field.sl_achieved_FR5 = number
field.sl_achieved_FR6 = number
field.sl_achieved_FR7 = number
field.measurement_date = time
field.assessor_ref = string
field.findings_count = number
field.sl_a_measurement_in_progress_ref = string
```

## Step 4 - Exception register

```ini
[iec_62443_sl_exception_register_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.exception_id = string
field.zone_id = string
field.fr_index = string
field.sl_target = number
field.sl_achieved = number
field.compensating_control_ref = string
field.business_acceptance_ref = string
field.expiry_at = time
```

## Step 5 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.63.7: IEC 62443-3-2 SL Conformance]
cron_schedule = 0 7 * * 2
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.63.7,reg=IEC-61511,clause=IEC-62443-3-2
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 6 - Functional-safety-specific notes

- **SL-T-allocation discipline**: the CRA team should resist the temptation to set every FR SL-T at 3 — over-allocation creates costly cap-ex without genuine risk-reduction. Anchor SL-T to the residual-risk-after-design from the LOPA.
- **Compensating control quality**: an exception with a compensating-control should require an explicit residual-risk re-calculation showing the compensating-control achieves equivalent risk-reduction to the missing SL.
grandmaExplanation: International standards rate every part of a safety system on seven cyber-security capabilities, and how strong the protection needs to be. We check every week that every required protection is in place, and if not, that a documented compensating control is approved and active.
